input.content:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
With the server building and static files in place, wire up a real notes API backed by SQLite. The full contract lives in /app/docs/api_contract.md; follow that document for status codes, headers, JSON shapes, pagination, body size limits, and error bodies.

Persist data in /app/data/notes.db. Load the API secret from /app/config/api.key, trim whitespace, and require it in X-API-Key on POST, PUT, and DELETE. Reads must work without the key; wrong or missing keys on writes are unauthorized. Apply the same title validation rules on PUT as on POST, including rejecting empty titles on updates. Collection GET must support limit and offset paging and expose the pre-page total in the X-Total-Count header as spelled out in the contract file.

Keep JSON content type and CORS headers on API responses, honor OPTIONS preflight as described in the contract, and make sure concurrent writes do not drop rows when verifiers hammer the service in parallel. Leave static serving intact so /app.js and the rest of /app/www still come through notes_server on port 8080 when you are done, and tell me when the API work is complete before you start the browser script.


Current terminal state:
New Terminal Output:

root@bb40ba43d60f:/app#

Current Terminal Screen:

SQLite access must remain correct under concurrent requests. Verifiers may reset /app/data/notes.db and send bursts of about fifteen parallel POST requests; eve
ry stored row must still appear in responses afterward.
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# sed -n '200,400p' /app/docs/api_contract.md
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# sed -n '400,800p' /app/docs/api_contract.md
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# cat /app/package.json
cat: /app/package.json: No such file or directory
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# ls -la /app/config /app/data /app/www 2>/dev/null || true
/app/config:
total 8
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root  41 May 26 12:40 .
drwxr-xr-x 10 root root 157 May 26 12:44 ..
-rw-r--r--  1 root root  26 May 26 12:40 api.key
-rw-r--r--  1 root root  89 May 26 12:40 defaults.env

/app/data:
total 12
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root    22 May 26 12:45 .
drwxr-xr-x 10 root root   157 May 26 12:44 ..
-rw-r--r--  1 root root 12288 May 26 12:45 notes.db

/app/www:
total 12
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root   55 May 26 12:40 .
drwxr-xr-x 10 root root  157 May 26 12:44 ..
-rw-r--r--  1 root root  943 May 26 12:40 app.js
-rw-r--r--  1 root root  643 May 26 12:40 index.html
-rw-r--r--  1 root root 1009 May 26 12:40 style.css
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# cat /app/config/api.key 2>/dev/null || true
tb3_notes_api_secret_2026
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# ls -la /app | grep -E 'server|app|index|main|notes' || true
-rwxr-xr-x  1 root root 18360 May 26 12:44 notes_server
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# find /app -maxdepth 2 -type f -iname '*server*' -o -iname 'index.*' -o -iname 'main.*' -o -iname 'app.*' | xargs -r ls -la
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 18360 May 26 12:44 /app/notes_server
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  5437 May 26 12:44 /app/src/main.c
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root   943 May 26 12:40 /app/www/app.js
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root   643 May 26 12:40 /app/www/index.html
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# node -v && npm -v
bash: node: command not found
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app#

Current Terminal Screen:
        PORT,
        NULL, NULL,
        &handle_request, NULL,
        MHD_OPTION_NOTIFY_COMPLETED, &request_completed, NULL,
        MHD_OPTION_END);

    if (!daemon_handle) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to start server\n");
        return 1;
    }

    printf("Server running on port %d\n", PORT);
    while (1) pause();
    return 0;
}
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# sed -n '260,520p' /app/src/main.c
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# sed -n '520,900p' /app/src/main.c
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# file /app/notes_server
/app/notes_server: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=7c736b0c7
188801e7b35d1a4379880943c101636, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, not stripped
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# ldd /app/notes_server || true
        linux-vdso.so.1 (0x00007ffe500cf000)
        libmicrohttpd.so.12 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libmicrohttpd.so.12 (0x00007f5f2a29e000)
        libsqlite3.so.0 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libsqlite3.so.0 (0x00007f5f2a11e000)
        libc.so.6 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6 (0x00007f5f29f2a000)
        libgnutls.so.30 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgnutls.so.30 (0x00007f5f29d01000)
        libm.so.6 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libm.so.6 (0x00007f5f29c0f000)
        /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2 (0x00007f5f2a2e2000)
        libp11-kit.so.0 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libp11-kit.so.0 (0x00007f5f29a6e000)
        libidn2.so.0 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libidn2.so.0 (0x00007f5f29a3b000)
        libunistring.so.5 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libunistring.so.5 (0x00007f5f29853000)
        libtasn1.so.6 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libtasn1.so.6 (0x00007f5f2983d000)
        libhogweed.so.6 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libhogweed.so.6 (0x00007f5f297f0000)
        libnettle.so.8 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libnettle.so.8 (0x00007f5f2979a000)
        libgmp.so.10 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgmp.so.10 (0x00007f5f29710000)
        libffi.so.8 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libffi.so.8 (0x00007f5f29703000)
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# sqlite3 /app/data/notes.db '.schema' 2>/dev/null || echo 'no sqlite3 cli'
no sqlite3 cli
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# which sqlite3 || true
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app#

Current Terminal Screen:
    }

    daemon_handle = MHD_start_daemon(
        MHD_USE_THREAD_PER_CONNECTION,
        PORT,
        NULL, NULL,
        &handle_request, NULL,
        MHD_OPTION_NOTIFY_COMPLETED, &request_completed, NULL,
        MHD_OPTION_END);

    if (!daemon_handle) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to start server\n");
        return 1;
    }

    printf("Server running on port %d\n", PORT);
    while (1) pause();
    return 0;
}
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# cat /app/Makefile
.RECIPEPREFIX := >
CC = gcc
CFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -O2 -Iinclude
LDFLAGS = -lmicrohttpd -lsqlite3

SOURCES = \
  src/main.c \
  src/routes.c \
  src/static.c \
  src/db.c \
  src/auth.c

TARGET = notes_server

$(TARGET): $(SOURCES)
>$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $(TARGET) $(SOURCES) $(LDFLAGS)

clean:
>rm -f $(TARGET)
root@bb40ba43d60f:/app#

root@bb40ba43d60f:/app# cat /app/Makefile
.RECIPEPREFIX := >
CC = gcc
CFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -O2 -Iinclude
LDFLAGS = -lmicrohttpd -lsqlite3

SOURCES = \
  src/main.c \
  src/routes.c \
  src/static.c \
  src/db.c \
  src/auth.c

TARGET = notes_server

$(TARGET): $(SOURCES)
>$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $(TARGET) $(SOURCES) $(LDFLAGS)

clean:
>rm -f $(TARGET)
